template: What is the sum of angles on a straight line?
explanation: Angles on a straight line always add up to 180°. This is known as the straight line angle property.

template: What is the sum of angles around a point?
explanation: Angles around a full point always add up to 360°.

template: Two angles lie on a straight line. One angle is {{a}}°. Find the other angle.
explanation: Angles on a straight line sum to 180°. So the missing angle = 180° − {{a}}° = {{180 - a}}°.

template: Two angles meet at a point. One angle is {{a}}°. Find the other angle.
explanation: Angles around a point sum to 360°. The missing angle = 360° − {{a}}° = {{360 - a}}°.

template: Two straight lines cross. One angle formed is {{a}}°. What is the vertically opposite angle?
explanation: Vertically opposite angles are equal. So the vertically opposite angle is also {{a}}°.

template: A triangle has angles of {{a}}° and {{b}}°. Find the third angle.
explanation: Angles in a triangle sum to 180°. Third angle = 180° − {{a}}° − {{b}}° = {{c}}°.

template: An exterior angle of a triangle is {{ext}}°. One of the two non-adjacent interior angles is {{a}}°. Find the other non-adjacent interior angle.
explanation: The exterior angle of a triangle equals the sum of the two non-adjacent interior angles. So the missing angle = {{ext}}° − {{a}}° = {{b}}°.

template: An isosceles triangle has a top angle of {{apex}}°. Find each base angle.
explanation: In an isosceles triangle, the two base angles are equal. Sum of base angles = 180° − {{apex}}° = {{180 - apex}}°. Each base angle = {{180 - apex}}° ÷ 2 = {{base}}°.

template: Two parallel lines are cut by a transversal. One alternate angle is {{a}}°. Find the other alternate angle.
explanation: Alternate angles (Z-angles) between parallel lines are equal. The other alternate angle is also {{a}}°.

template: Two parallel lines are cut by a transversal. One corresponding angle is {{a}}°. Find the other corresponding angle.
explanation: Corresponding angles (F-angles) between parallel lines are equal. The other corresponding angle is also {{a}}°.

template: Two parallel lines are cut by a transversal. One co-interior angle is {{a}}°. Find the other co-interior angle.
explanation: Co-interior angles (C-angles) between parallel lines add up to 180°. The other angle = 180° − {{a}}° = {{180 - a}}°.

template: Find the sum of the interior angles of a polygon with {{n}} sides.
explanation: The sum of interior angles of any polygon = (n − 2) × 180°. For {{n}} sides: ({{n}} − 2) × 180° = {{n - 2}} × 180° = {{sum}}°.

template: Find the interior angle of a regular polygon with {{n}} sides.
explanation: Interior angle of a regular polygon = (n − 2) × 180° ÷ n = ({{n}} − 2) × 180° ÷ {{n}} = {{angle}}°.

template: The exterior angle of a regular polygon is {{ext}}°. How many sides does it have?
explanation: Sum of exterior angles of any polygon = 360°. Number of sides = 360° ÷ {{ext}}° = {{n}}.

template: Find the area of a rectangle with length {{l}} cm and width {{w}} cm.
explanation: Area of a rectangle = length × width = {{l}} × {{w}} = {{area}} cm².

template: Find the area of a triangle with base {{b}} cm and perpendicular height {{h}} cm.
explanation: Area of a triangle = ½ × base × height = ½ × {{b}} × {{h}} = {{area}} cm².

template: A parallelogram has a base of {{b}} cm and a perpendicular height of {{h}} cm. Find its area.
explanation: Area of a parallelogram = base × perpendicular height = {{b}} × {{h}} = {{area}} cm².

template: A trapezium has parallel sides of {{a}} cm and {{b}} cm, and a perpendicular height of {{h}} cm. Find its area.
explanation: Area of a trapezium = ½ × (sum of parallel sides) × height = ½ × ({{a}} + {{b}}) × {{h}} = ½ × {{a + b}} × {{h}} = {{area}} cm².

template: Find the area of a circle with radius {{r}} cm. Give your answer to 2 decimal places.
explanation: Area = π × r² = π × {{r}}² = π × {{r * r}} ≈ {{area}} cm².

template: Find the circumference of a circle with diameter {{d}} cm. Give your answer to 2 decimal places.
explanation: Circumference = π × d = π × {{d}} ≈ {{circ}} cm.

template: A right-angled triangle has shorter sides of {{a}} cm and {{b}} cm. Find the hypotenuse. Give your answer to 2 decimal places.
explanation: Using Pythagoras: c² = a² + b² = {{a}}² + {{b}}² = {{a*a}} + {{b*b}} = {{a*a + b*b}}. So c = √{{a*a + b*b}} ≈ {{c}} cm.

template: A right-angled triangle has hypotenuse {{c}} cm and one shorter side {{a}} cm. Find the other shorter side. Give your answer to 2 decimal places.
explanation: Using Pythagoras: b² = c² − a² = {{c}}² − {{a}}² = {{c*c}} − {{a*a}} = {{c*c - a*a}}. So b = √{{c*c - a*a}} ≈ {{b}} cm.

template: A right-angled triangle has sides 3k, 4k and 5k. If k = {{k}}, find the hypotenuse.
explanation: The 3-4-5 right triangle is a Pythagorean triple. Scaling by k = {{k}}: hypotenuse = 5 × {{k}} = {{hyp}} cm.

template: In a right-angled triangle, the angle is {{ang}}° and the adjacent side is {{adj}} cm. Find the opposite side. Give your answer to 2 decimal places.
explanation: tan(angle) = opposite/adjacent, so opposite = adjacent × tan(angle) = {{adj}} × tan({{ang}}°) ≈ {{opp}} cm.

template: In a right-angled triangle, the angle is {{ang}}° and the opposite side is {{opp}} cm. Find the hypotenuse. Give your answer to 2 decimal places.
explanation: sin(angle) = opposite/hypotenuse, so hypotenuse = opposite/sin(angle) = {{opp}}/sin({{ang}}°) ≈ {{hyp}} cm.

template: In a right-angled triangle, the adjacent side is {{adj}} cm and the hypotenuse is {{hyp}} cm. Find the angle. Give your answer to 1 decimal place.
explanation: cos(angle) = adjacent/hypotenuse = {{adj}}/{{hyp}}. So angle = cos⁻¹({{adj}}/{{hyp}}) ≈ {{ang}}°.

template: Find the volume of a cuboid with length {{l}} cm, width {{w}} cm and height {{h}} cm.
explanation: Volume of a cuboid = length × width × height = {{l}} × {{w}} × {{h}} = {{vol}} cm³.

template: Find the total surface area of a cuboid with length {{l}} cm, width {{w}} cm and height {{h}} cm.
A triangular prism has a triangular cross-section with base {{b}} cm and height {{h}} cm. The prism is {{len}} cm long. Find its volume.
explanation: Volume of a prism = cross-sectional area × length. Area of triangle = ½ × {{b}} × {{h}} = {{0.5*b*h}} cm². Volume = {{0.5*b*h}} × {{len}} = {{vol}} cm³.

template: Find the volume of a cylinder with radius {{r}} cm and height {{h}} cm. Give your answer to 2 decimal places.
explanation: Volume of a cylinder = π × r² × h = π × {{r}}² × {{h}} = π × {{r*r}} × {{h}} ≈ {{vol}} cm³.

template: Find the volume of a cone with radius {{r}} cm and height {{h}} cm. Give your answer to 2 decimal places.
explanation: Volume of a cone = ⅓ × π × r² × h = ⅓ × π × {{r}}² × {{h}} ≈ {{vol}} cm³.

template: Find the volume of a sphere with radius {{r}} cm. Give your answer to 2 decimal places.
explanation: Volume of a sphere = (4/3)π r³ = (4/3) × π × {{r}}³ ≈ {{vol}} cm³.

template: Find the total surface area of a cylinder with radius {{r}} cm and height {{h}} cm. Give your answer to 2 decimal places.
A ship travels on a bearing of {{bear}}°. Write this bearing as a three-figure bearing.
explanation: Bearings are always written as three figures measured clockwise from North. {{bear}}° is written as {{bear < 10 ? '00' + bear : bear < 100 ? '0' + bear : '' + bear}}.

template: A ship sails on a bearing of {{bear}}°. What is the back bearing (bearing to return to start)?
explanation: The back bearing is the original bearing ± 180°. Since {{bear}}° is {{bear >= 180 ? 'greater than or equal to' : 'less than'}} 180°, back bearing = {{bear}}° {{bear >= 180 ? '−' : '+'}} 180° = {{back}}°.

template: Describe the locus of points equidistant from two fixed points A and B.
explanation: The set of all points equidistant from two fixed points lies on the perpendicular bisector of the line segment joining them.

template: Describe the locus of points exactly {{r}} cm from a fixed point P.

answerConfig.textAnswer: a circle of radius {{r}} cm centred at P
acceptedAnswers: a circle of radius {{r}} cm centred at P
circle with radius {{r}} cm at P

explanation: All points exactly {{r}} cm from a fixed point form a circle of radius {{r}} cm centred at P.

template: Two similar triangles have corresponding sides. The first triangle has sides {{a1}} cm and {{b1}} cm. The second triangle has the side corresponding to {{a1}} as {{a2}} cm. Find the side corresponding to {{b1}}.
explanation: Scale factor = {{a2}}/{{a1}} = {{k}}. The corresponding side = {{b1}} × {{k}} = {{b2}} cm.

template: The angle at the centre of a circle is {{cent}}°. Find the inscribed angle (angle at the circumference) subtended by the same arc.
explanation: The angle at the centre is twice the angle at the circumference when both are subtended by the same arc. Inscribed angle = {{cent}}° ÷ 2 = {{inscribed}}°.

template: A triangle is inscribed in a circle with one side as the diameter. What is the angle at the opposite vertex?
explanation: The angle in a semicircle is always 90°. Any angle inscribed in a semicircle (with the diameter as its base) is a right angle.

template: In a cyclic quadrilateral, one angle is {{ang}}°. Find the opposite angle.
explanation: Opposite angles in a cyclic quadrilateral add up to 180°. The opposite angle = 180° − {{ang}}° = {{opp}}°.

template: A tangent meets a radius at the point of contact. One angle in the diagram is {{ang}}°. Find the angle between the tangent and the radius.
explanation: A tangent to a circle is always perpendicular to the radius at the point of contact. The angle between a tangent and a radius is always 90°.

template: When reflecting a shape, which property is preserved?
explanation: A reflection is an isometry — it preserves size and shape (congruence). However, it reverses the orientation (flips the shape).

template: To fully describe a rotation, which three pieces of information are needed?
explanation: To fully describe a rotation you need: (1) the centre of rotation, (2) the angle of rotation, and (3) the direction (clockwise or anticlockwise).

template: A shape is enlarged with scale factor {{k}} from a given centre. A side of the original shape is {{s}} cm. Find the length of the corresponding side in the image.
explanation: Under an enlargement with scale factor {{k}}, all lengths are multiplied by {{k}}. New side = {{s}} × {{k}} = {{img}} cm.

template: A shape has area {{area}} cm². It is enlarged with linear scale factor {{k}}. Find the area of the enlarged shape.
explanation: When a shape is enlarged with linear scale factor k, its area is multiplied by k². New area = {{area}} × {{k}}² = {{area}} × {{k*k}} = {{newArea}} cm².

template: Vector **a** = ({{ax}}, {{ay}}) and vector **b** = ({{bx}}, {{by}}). Find **a** + **b**.
explanation: To add vectors, add corresponding components: **a** + **b** = ({{ax}} + {{bx}}, {{ay}} + {{by}}) = ({{rx}}, {{ry}}).

template: Find the magnitude of the vector ({{vx}}, {{vy}}). Give your answer to 2 decimal places.
explanation: Magnitude = √(x² + y²) = √({{vx}}² + {{vy}}²) = √({{vx*vx}} + {{vy*vy}}) = √{{vx*vx + vy*vy}} ≈ {{mag}}.

template: Find the midpoint of the line segment joining ({{x1}}, {{y1}}) and ({{x2}}, {{y2}}).
Find the gradient of the line passing through ({{x1}}, {{y1}}) and ({{x2}}, {{y2}}).
A line has gradient {{m}} and y-intercept {{c}}. Write the equation of the line.
explanation: The equation of a line is y = mx + c, where m is the gradient and c is the y-intercept. Here, y = {{m}}x + {{c}}.

template: A line has gradient {{m}}. Find the gradient of the line perpendicular to it.
explanation: For perpendicular lines, the product of their gradients = −1. Perpendicular gradient = −1/{{m}} = {{perp}}.

template: Find the arc length of a sector with radius {{r}} cm and angle {{theta}}°. Give your answer to 2 decimal places.
explanation: Arc length = (θ/360) × 2πr = ({{theta}}/360) × 2π × {{r}} ≈ {{arc}} cm.

template: Find the area of a sector with radius {{r}} cm and angle {{theta}}°. Give your answer to 2 decimal places.
explanation: Sector area = (θ/360) × πr² = ({{theta}}/360) × π × {{r}}² ≈ {{area}} cm².

template: Find the length of the space diagonal of a cuboid with length {{l}} cm, width {{w}} cm and height {{h}} cm. Give your answer to 2 decimal places.
explanation: Space diagonal = √(l² + w² + h²) = √({{l}}² + {{w}}² + {{h}}²) = √({{l*l}} + {{w*w}} + {{h*h}}) = √{{l*l + w*w + h*h}} ≈ {{diag}} cm.

template: A boat sails {{dist}} km on a bearing of {{bear}}°. How far north (or south) has it travelled? Give your answer to 2 decimal places.
explanation: The northward component = distance × cos(bearing) = {{dist}} × cos({{bear}}°) ≈ {{northComp}} km. (Negative cos indicates south.)

template: The lines y = {{m1}}x + {{c1}} and y = {{m2}}x + {{c2}} are drawn on a graph. Find the x-coordinate of their intersection.
explanation: Set equations equal: {{m1}}x + {{c1}} = {{m2}}x + {{c2}}. Rearranging: {{m1 - m2}}x = {{c2 - c1}}, so x = {{c2 - c1}}/{{m1 - m2}} = {{xint}}.

template: Each interior angle of a regular polygon is {{intAng}}°. How many sides does the polygon have?
explanation: Exterior angle = 180° − {{intAng}}° = {{180 - intAng}}°. Number of sides = 360° ÷ {{180 - intAng}}° = {{n}}.

template: Which of the following correctly describes alternate angles?

options: Equal angles on opposite sides of a transversal between parallel lines
Equal angles on the same side of a transversal
Angles at the same position at each intersection
answerConfig.answer: Equal angles on opposite sides of a transversal between parallel lines

explanation: Alternate angles are equal and lie on opposite sides of the transversal, between two parallel lines. They form a Z-shape.

template: Two triangles have two equal sides and the included angle between those sides is also equal. Which congruence condition applies?
explanation: SAS (Side-Angle-Side) states that if two sides and the included angle of one triangle equal those of another, the triangles are congruent. The 'included angle' is the angle between the two known sides.

template: Two similar shapes have corresponding lengths in the ratio {{p}}:{{q}}. Find the ratio of their areas.
explanation: If the linear scale factor is {{p}}:{{q}}, the area scale factor is {{p}}²:{{q}}² = {{ap}}:{{aq}}.

template: Two similar solids have corresponding lengths in the ratio {{p}}:{{q}}. Find the ratio of their volumes.
explanation: If the linear scale factor is {{p}}:{{q}}, the volume scale factor is {{p}}³:{{q}}³ = {{vp}}:{{vq}}.

template: From an external point P, a tangent touches a circle of radius {{r}} cm at point T. The distance from P to the centre O is {{d}} cm. Find the length of the tangent PT. Give your answer to 2 decimal places.
explanation: Since OT is a radius and PT is a tangent, angle OTP = 90°. By Pythagoras: PT² = OP² − OT² = {{d}}² − {{r}}² = {{d*d}} − {{r*r}} = {{d*d - r*r}}. So PT = √{{d*d - r*r}} ≈ {{pt}} cm.

template: Find the perimeter of a sector with radius {{r}} cm and angle {{theta}}°. Give your answer to 2 decimal places.
explanation: Perimeter of a sector = arc length + 2 radii = ({{theta}}/360) × 2π × {{r}} + 2 × {{r}} ≈ {{arc}} + {{2*r}} = {{perim}} cm.

template: A circle of radius {{r}} cm sits inside a square of side {{s}} cm (the circle touches all four sides). Find the shaded area between the circle and the square. Give your answer to 2 decimal places.
explanation: Area of square = {{s}}² = {{s*s}} cm². Area of circle = π × {{r}}² ≈ {{round(3.14159265358979 * r * r, 2)}} cm². Shaded area = {{s*s}} − {{round(3.14159265358979 * r * r, 2)}} ≈ {{shaded}} cm².

template: An annulus (ring) has outer radius {{R}} cm and inner radius {{r}} cm. Find its area. Give your answer to 2 decimal places.
explanation: Area of annulus = π(R² − r²) = π({{R}}² − {{r}}²) = π × {{R*R - r*r}} ≈ {{area}} cm².

template: In triangle ABC, angle A = {{A}}°, angle B = {{B}}°, and side a (opposite angle A) = {{a}} cm. Find side b (opposite angle B) to 2 decimal places.
explanation: By the Sine Rule: a/sin(A) = b/sin(B). So b = a × sin(B)/sin(A) = {{a}} × sin({{B}}°)/sin({{A}}°) ≈ {{b}} cm.
